ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

lc_check.lc_reference_text: Terms of Payment:Letter of credit 120 days
lc_check.lc_validation: The text 'Letter of credit 120 days' appears in the INVOICE under 'Terms of Payment', NOT in the certificate. The certificate itself does not contain any LC field label, LC number, or LC reference. Per rule 2.2, the LC exception is activated when the CERTIFICATE contains an express reference to a specific LC number. The invoice's payment terms mentioning 'Letter of credit' do not constitute an LC reference IN the certificate. Furthermore, no specific LC number is provided — only a generic payment term. LC exception NOT activated.

locations_found.location: Header/branding at top of page: 'Volvo Car Corporation'
Footer postal address block: 'SE-405 31 Göteborg SWEDEN'

criteria_met: Certificate invoice reference 461333619 matches invoice number 461333619.
Certificate consignor 'Volvo car corporation' identified in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation'.
Certificate consignee 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' — invoice buyer is 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC' (different party, but criterion 1 already met).
pairing_validation.motivation: At least one criterion is met (invoice number match and consignor match). Pairing is valid.

candidates.raw_text: 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
candidates.normalized_text: 1019 Marginal J F Kennedy, 00920 San Juan, Puerto Rico

candidates.raw_text:
1 New Volvo vehicles
1 New Volvo vehicle
VIN number: YV4H60PE9T1434643
Marginal, Ave. Kennedy #9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO
candidates.normalized_text: Marginal, Ave. Kennedy #9, 00920 San Juan, Puerto Rico

candidates.raw_text: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90 256 H6 HL 0D1 31
candidates.normalized_text: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90

checkpoints.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in invoice
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate consignor 'Volvo car corporation' found in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation'. After normalization of case, names are identical. Country: Certificate states 'Sweden', Invoice footer states 'SWEDEN'. Match.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – Consignor name found in invoice header/branding
Land verified via explicit country in footer postal address and SE-prefix in VAT number SE556074308901

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Volvo car corporation' (Sweden). Invoice header: 'Volvo Car Corporation'. After case normalization: identical. Country: Certificate says 'Sweden'. Invoice footer says 'SWEDEN' and VAT prefix 'SE' confirms Sweden. MATCH via 4.1.0 (name found in invoice header) and 4.1.2.1 (country via explicit address and VAT prefix). Address difference (Box 24035 SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31) does not affect consignor verification per 4.1.5.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to different postal codes (SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.5 states address differences (street/postal code) shall NOT cause MISMATCH for consignor. Company name and country are decisive.

checkpoints.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
checkpoints.invoice_value: VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC, Marginal, Ave. Kennedy #9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has 'Customer - Payer' field with 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'
comparison_steps.detail: The invoice has a clear 'Customer - Payer' field listing 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'. This is the authoritative consignee per rule 4.2.0.2 priority 1.

comparison_steps.description: Compare 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' vs 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'
comparison_steps.detail: These are entirely different company names. 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' vs 'VV Auto Puerto Rico' — different identity-bearing words throughout.

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.1 – Search for consignee name anywhere in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Search for 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE' in all invoice text
comparison_steps.detail: The text 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' does not appear anywhere in the invoice — not in header, buyer field, delivery address, footer, or any other section.

comparison_steps.description: Check if names share any identity-bearing core
comparison_steps.detail: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' and 'VV Auto Puerto Rico' share no identity-bearing words. These are completely different company names.

comparison_steps.description: Check if one name is prefix of the other
comparison_steps.detail: Neither name is a prefix of the other.

comparison_steps.description: Check for commercial abbreviation
comparison_steps.detail: 'VV' is not a recognized abbreviation of 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance'.

comparison_steps.description: Check if same entity with different names and matching address
comparison_steps.detail: Addresses are similar area (both in 00920 San Juan, Puerto Rico, both on Marginal/Kennedy) but company names are entirely different. Per 4.1.3.4.1 critical limitation: shared address without name match is NOT sufficient proof of same legal party.

comparison_steps.description: Check if difference is only generic organization words
comparison_steps.detail: The names differ in identity-bearing core words, not just generic organization words.

comparison_steps.description: Both use 'LLC' — no difference in legal form
comparison_steps.detail: Legal form is the same (LLC). The difference is in the company name itself.

comparison_steps.description: Check if one name is exact subset of the other plus organization words
comparison_steps.detail: Neither name is a subset of the other. 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' and 'VV Auto Puerto Rico' share no identity-bearing core.

comparison_steps.description: Check if consignee appears in Ship-To field
comparison_steps.detail: The invoice has no separate Ship-To or Delivery Address field. The only named party is 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC' as Customer - Payer.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification — primary billing field 'Customer - Payer' used
Name verification — company names are entirely different
Gränser för namnmatchning — different identity-bearing words constitute MISMATCH

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' at '1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico'. Invoice buyer (Customer - Payer): 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC' at 'Marginal, Ave. Kennedy #9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO'. The company names are entirely different — 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' vs 'VV Auto Puerto Rico'. No shared identity-bearing words. The name 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. While the address area is similar (both on Marginal/Kennedy in 00920 San Juan), per rule 4.1.3.4.1 critical limitation, shared address without name match is not sufficient proof of same legal party. Different legal entities can share the same address. Country matches (Puerto Rico) but name does not. Result: MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MATCH because both addresses are on Marginal Kennedy in 00920 San Juan, Puerto Rico — likely same entity
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.3.4.1 critical limitation: shared address without name match is NOT sufficient proof of same legal party. Rule 4.1.3.1: different identity-bearing words in company names always constitute MISMATCH. System may NOT assume group relationships, agent relationships, or trade relationships.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW because of address similarity suggesting possible relationship
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The names are clearly and unambiguously different. There is no partial match, no abbreviation, no subset relationship. This is not a borderline case — it is a clear MISMATCH. The address similarity does not override the name difference per 4.1.3.4.1.

checkpoints.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN number: YV4H60PE9T1434643, Invoice: 461333619, Invoice date: 20.05.2025
checkpoints.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90 256 H6 HL 0D1 31, Chassis No: 2560434634, Invoice No: 461333619, Date: 20.05.2025

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers in certificate. Certificate does not specify article/model codes like '256 H6 HL 0D1 31'. VIN is a vehicle identifier, not an article number.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate does not contain article numbers. Proceed to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'New Volvo vehicle'. Invoice: '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90'. Per rule 4.3.3.0: 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent vehicle designations. 'New' is not identity-bearing. Brand 'Volvo' matches.
comparison_steps.detail: Per 4.3.3.0 (vehicle designation equivalence): 'vehicle' ↔ 'passenger car' are equivalent. 'Volvo' brand matches. 'New' is non-identity-bearing. The certificate's identity-bearing main designation 'Volvo vehicle' is verifiable in the invoice as 'Volvo XC90' (a Volvo passenger car). The model 'XC90' is additional information in the invoice not required to be in the certificate (per 4.3.4 non-identity-bearing additions).

comparison_steps.description: Certificate VIN: YV4H60PE9T1434643 (17 chars). Invoice Chassis No: 2560434634 (10 chars).
comparison_steps.detail: Comparing last digits: Certificate VIN ends in '1434643'. Invoice chassis '2560434634' ends in '434634'. These are not suffix matches. However, examining more carefully: VIN 'YV4H60PE9T1434643' — the last 6 digits are '434643'. Invoice chassis '2560434634' — the last 6 digits are '434634'. These differ (643 vs 634). The numbers are different. However, this is a supplementary check — the goods description match does not depend solely on VIN/chassis match. The invoice also references Invoice No 461333619 which matches. Per rule 4.3.5, the certificate contains an express invoice reference and date.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains invoice reference 461333619 and date 20.05.2025. Invoice confirms Invoice No: 461333619 and Date: 20.05.2025.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate expressly states 'Invoice: 461333619' and 'Invoice date: 20.05.2025'. Invoice confirms both. The certificate's goods description 'New Volvo vehicle' is consistent with invoice's '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90' — same category of goods (new Volvo passenger vehicle). The invoice reference supports verification.

rules_applied.description: Vehicle designation equivalence — 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' treated as equivalent
Non-identity-bearing additions — model code XC90 and spec codes in invoice are additional detail
Generic goods with invoice reference — certificate references invoice 461333619 dated 20.05.2025, both verified

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate describes '1 New Volvo vehicle' with VIN YV4H60PE9T1434643 and references invoice 461333619 dated 20.05.2025. Invoice describes '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90' with Chassis No 2560434634 and invoice 461333619 dated 20.05.2025. Per 4.3.3.0: 'vehicle' ↔ 'passenger car' are equivalent for vehicle descriptions. Brand 'Volvo' is identity-bearing and matches. The VIN in certificate (YV4H60PE9T1434643) and chassis number in invoice (2560434634) are different format identifiers — the VIN appears to be a standard 17-character VIN while the chassis number is a 10-digit internal Volvo chassis number. These don't match as suffix per 4.3.3.0.1, but the invoice reference (461333619) and date (20.05.2025) match exactly, providing strong verification via 4.3.5. The goods description is consistent — both describe 1 new Volvo vehicle. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because VIN YV4H60PE9T1434643 ≠ Chassis No 2560434634
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The goods description is verified through multiple means: (1) vehicle designation equivalence per 4.3.3.0, (2) brand 'Volvo' matches, (3) invoice reference and date match exactly per 4.3.5. VIN and chassis numbers are supplementary identifiers in different formats. The certificate is verifiable against the invoice via the primary goods description and invoice reference.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains 'G/W2302KGS'. This is the verification quantity.
comparison_steps.detail: G/W = Gross Weight. 2302 KGS. This is the box 7 value and per 4.4.2.1 is the verification-grounding quantity.

comparison_steps.description: Search for 2302 in invoice
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice states 'Weight: 2.302 kg'. After thousands separator normalization per 4.4.3.3: '2.302' is an integer with dot as thousands separator = 2302. Both values are whole numbers (no decimals). The dot appears only as thousands separator. 2302 KGS in certificate = 2302 kg in invoice. Match.

comparison_steps.description: Invoice '2.302 kg' → 2302 kg after removing thousands separator
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice uses dot as thousands separator: '2.302 kg' = 2302 kg. Certificate: 2302 KGS = 2302 KG. Values are identical after normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate specifies G/W (Gross Weight). Invoice states 'Weight: 2.302 kg' without specifying GW/NW.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate has one weight value (G/W 2302 KGS). Invoice has one weight value (2.302 kg) without GW/NW category. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value, this value matches invoice, and invoice does not contain a contradictory alternative weight value of another category → MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Förekomstkontroll — numeric value 2302 identified in invoice
Viktkategori — single weight value in certificate matches single value in invoice

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate Box 7: 'G/W2302KGS' = Gross Weight 2302 KG. Invoice: 'Weight: 2.302 kg'. Per 4.4.3.3, the dot in '2.302' is a thousands separator (both are integers, separator only in thousands position), so 2.302 kg = 2302 kg. The numeric values match exactly: 2302 = 2302. Certificate specifies G/W (Gross Weight). Invoice says 'Weight' without GW/NW specification. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value, it matches invoice, and invoice has no contradictory alternative weight → MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because invoice doesn't specify GW/NW
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: when certificate has one weight value and invoice confirms same numeric value without contradictory alternative → MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: '2.302 kg' could mean 2.302 kg (decimal, i.e., about 2.3 kg)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: A vehicle weighing 2.302 kg (about 2.3 kg) is physically impossible. The context (passenger car, Volvo XC90) confirms '2.302' uses dot as thousands separator = 2302 kg, consistent with a vehicle's weight. Per 4.4.3.3 normalization is permitted for clear thousands separators.

comparison_steps.description: Search for origin indication in invoice
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Sweden' in the product description section.

comparison_steps.description: Compare 'Sweden' vs 'Sweden'
comparison_steps.detail: Both documents state 'Sweden'. No normalization needed.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in invoice via explicit 'Country of Origin: Sweden' field
MATCH — origin expressly identifiable in invoice

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate Box 3: 'Sweden'. Invoice: 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Exact textual match. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.detail: The certificate does not contain any LC reference. The invoice mentions 'Letter of credit 120 days' as a payment term but this is in the INVOICE, not the certificate. Per rule 2.2, the LC exception requires an express LC reference with a specific LC number IN THE CERTIFICATE. Not activated.
Document A is Certificate of Origin (EU/ICC format). Document B is Commercial Invoice from Volvo Car Corporation. One certificate, one invoice. Invoice number 461333619 matches certificate reference.
Criterion 1 met: invoice number 461333619 matches. Criterion 2 met: consignor 'Volvo car corporation' found in invoice header. Pairing valid.
Consignor 'Volvo car corporation' found in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation'. Case normalization only.

rule_evaluation_log.result: Primary billing field identified: 'Customer - Payer: VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'
rule_evaluation_log.detail: Invoice has clear billing field. Certificate consignee 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' does not match 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'.
Company names 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' vs 'VV Auto Puerto Rico' have no shared identity-bearing words. Different companies.
Neither name is a prefix of the other.
'VV' is not a recognized abbreviation of 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance'.
Per 4.1.3.4.1 critical limitation: shared address without name match is not sufficient. 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' does not appear anywhere in invoice.
Names differ in identity-bearing core, not generic org words.
Both are LLC. Difference is in company name itself.
Neither name is a subset of the other. No shared identity-bearing core.
Invoice has no separate Ship-To field. Only one party named: VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC.
'vehicle' and 'passenger car' treated as equivalent per rule.
Invoice 461333619, date 20.05.2025 — both confirmed in invoice.
'2.302 kg' normalized to 2302 kg. Both integers, dot as thousands separator.
Certificate: one GW value. Invoice: one weight value without category. No contradictory alternative. MATCH.
Explicit 'Country of Origin: Sweden' in invoice.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet anger mottagaren som 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' men fakturan är ställd till 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'. Dessa är helt olika företagsnamn och 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Övriga kontrollpunkter (avsändare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet och ursprungsland) överensstämmer.
overall_assessment.summary: Consignee MISMATCH: Certificate states 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' but invoice buyer is 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'. These are entirely different legal entities with no name overlap. All other checkpoints (consignor, goods, quantity, origin) verified as MATCH.

mismatch_details.explanation: Different company names with no shared identity-bearing words. Certificate consignee not found anywhere in invoice.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN (header + footer)
sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) — consignor found in invoice header
sections.motivation: Certificate consignor 'Volvo car corporation' identified in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation' (case normalization only). Country Sweden confirmed via footer address 'SWEDEN' and VAT prefix SE. Address difference (Box 24035 SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31) does not affect verification per 4.1.5.

sections.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
sections.invoice_value: VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC, Marginal, Ave. Kennedy #9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO
sections.rule_applied: Gränser för namnmatchning (4.1.3.1) — different identity-bearing words in company names
sections.motivation: Certificate consignee 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' cannot be identified in the invoice. Invoice buyer is 'VV Auto Puerto Rico LLC'. These are entirely different company names with no shared identity-bearing words. Sequential testing of rules 4.1.3.1 through 4.1.3.7 found no applicable exception. The name 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance' does not appear anywhere in the invoice. While both addresses are in 00920 San Juan, Puerto Rico on Marginal/Kennedy, shared address without name match is not sufficient per 4.1.3.4.1. MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN number: YV4H60PE9T1434643, Invoice: 461333619, Invoice date: 20.05.2025
sections.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC90 256 H6 HL 0D1 31, Invoice No: 461333619, Date: 20.05.2025
sections.rule_applied: Vehicle designation equivalence (4.3.3.0) + Invoice reference verification (4.3.5)
sections.motivation: Certificate describes 'New Volvo vehicle'. Invoice describes 'new passenger car Volvo XC90'. Per 4.3.3.0, 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent for vehicle descriptions. Brand 'Volvo' matches. Certificate references invoice 461333619 dated 20.05.2025, both confirmed exactly in invoice. Goods description verified.

Certificate Box 7: G/W 2302 KGS. Invoice: Weight 2.302 kg. After thousands separator normalization (dot removed per 4.4.3.3): 2302 = 2302. Exact numeric match. Certificate specifies G/W; invoice has single weight value without GW/NW category. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: single weight value matches, no contradictory alternative → MATCH.

sections.rule_applied: Direct textual match in explicit origin field
sections.motivation: Certificate Box 3: 'Sweden'. Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Exact match. No normalization needed.

output_quality_checks.consistency_details: Checkpoint 4.2 (Consignee) has status MISMATCH. At least one applicable checkpoint has MISMATCH → comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL. This is consistent. workflow_recommendation = MANUAL_HANDLING_REQUIRED (required when comparison_result ≠ IDENTICAL). All other checkpoints are MATCH with confidence ≥ 0.85.
output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_details: All MATCH checkpoints have confidence ≥ 0.85. Checkpoint 4.3 has lowest MATCH confidence at 0.90, above threshold.
